title: althaea agrigentina
intro: Althaea agrigentina, also known as the Sardinian marsh mallow, is a perennial plant species in the mallow family (Malvaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which make it effective in treating digestive issues, wounds, and skin irritations. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its mucilaginous roots, which provide relief from coughs, sore throats, and gastrointestinal problems. Althaea agrigentina can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used in the treatment of different health issues.
